message:
feat: add robot mode for AI agent integration

This feature enables AI coding agents (Claude Code, Codex, Gemini, etc.)
to programmatically interact with NTM through machine-readable JSON output.

**New Package: internal/robot/**

Core types:
- SessionInfo: Session metadata with agent list, window count, attached state
- Agent: Agent type (claude/codex/gemini/cursor/windsurf/aider), pane info,
  window index, activity status
- StatusOutput: Complete system status including all sessions, summary stats
- PlanOutput: Recommended actions based on current state

Functions:
- PrintStatus(): JSON output of all sessions with agent counts and details
- PrintPlan(): Execution plan with recommended next actions
- PrintSessions(): Minimal session list for quick queries
- PrintVersion(): Version info as structured JSON
- PrintHelp(): Documentation for robot mode usage

Agent detection:
- Parses pane titles and running commands to identify agent types
- Supports: claude, codex, gemini, cursor, windsurf, aider
- Falls back to "user" for unrecognized panes

**CLI Integration (internal/cli/root.go)**

New flags:
- --robot-help: Show AI agent documentation in JSON format
- --robot-status: Output complete session status as JSON
- --robot-version: Output version info as JSON
- --robot-plan: Output execution plan recommendations

Enhanced version command:
- Added --short/-s flag for version number only
- Extended output: commit hash, build date, builder info
- Version info synced to robot package via init()

Build info variables:
- Added Commit, Date, BuiltBy alongside existing Version
- Set via goreleaser ldflags during release builds

This enables AI agents to:
1. Discover existing sessions and their agents
2. Plan multi-agent workflows programmatically
3. Monitor session state without parsing human-readable output
4. Integrate NTM into automated pipelines

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"ntm-8h3.1","title":"File Context Injection (--file flag)","description":"Add --file flag to ntm send to inject file contents into prompts.

## Use Case
Instead of manually copying file contents, specify files to include:
```bash
ntm send myproject --all \
  --file=src/auth.py \
  --file=src/models.py \
  "Refactor these to use dependency injection"
```

## Prompt Injection Format
Files are prepended to prompt with clear formatting:
```
# File: src/auth.py
\`\`\`python
<contents of src/auth.py>
\`\`\`

# File: src/models.py
\`\`\`python
<contents of src/models.py>
\`\`\`

---

Refactor these to use dependency injection
```

## Extended Syntax
```bash
--file=path                    # Entire file
--file=path:10-50              # Lines 10-50
--file=path:10-                # Line 10 to end
--file=path:-50                # First 50 lines
--file=path:func:my_function   # Extract function (future)
```

## Implementation
```go
type FileSpec struct {
    Path      string
    StartLine int  // 0 = from beginning
    EndLine   int  // 0 = to end
}

func ParseFileSpec(spec string) (FileSpec, error) {
    // Parse "path:start-end" format
}

func InjectFiles(specs []FileSpec, prompt string) (string, error) {
    var parts []string
    
    for _, spec := range specs {
        content, err := readFileRange(spec)
        if err != nil {
            return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to read %s: %w", spec.Path, err)
        }
        
        lang := detectLanguage(spec.Path)
        header := fmt.Sprintf("# File: %s", spec.Path)
        if spec.StartLine > 0 || spec.EndLine > 0 {
            header += fmt.Sprintf(" (lines %d-%d)", spec.StartLine, spec.EndLine)
        }
        
        parts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf("%s\n```%s\n%s\n```", 
            header, lang, content))
    }
    
    parts = append(parts, "---\n\n"+prompt)
    return strings.Join(parts, "\n\n"), nil
}
```

## Language Detection
Map file extensions to markdown language tags:
```go
var langMap = map[string]string{
    ".py":   "python",
    ".go":   "go",
    ".js":   "javascript",
    ".ts":   "typescript",
    ".rs":   "rust",
    ".rb":   "ruby",
    ".java": "java",
    // etc.
}
```

## Integration with Templates
Templates can reference {{file}} and {{files}}:
```markdown
---
name: review
variables:
  file: required
---
Review the following file for issues:

{{file}}

Focus on security and performance.
```

## Flags
| Flag | Description |
|------|-------------|
| -f, --file | File to include (repeatable) |
| --file-format | Format: markdown (default), raw, xml |
| --no-file-header | Omit file path headers |

## Edge Cases
- File not found: Error with clear message
- Binary files: Skip with warning
- Very large files: Warn if > 50KB, --force to override
- Relative vs absolute paths: Resolve relative to cwd

## Files to Modify
- internal/cli/send.go - Add --file flag processing
- internal/prompt/inject.go - File injection logic (new)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-10T02:43:58.519465-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T02:43:58.519465-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-8h3.1","depends_on_id":"ntm-8h3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-10T02:43:58.520574-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"ntm-9sw","title":"Add --json flag to spawn, create, add commands","description":"","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-10T02:39:53.508861-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T02:39:53.508861-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-9sw","depends_on_id":"ntm-517","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-10T02:40:13.265774-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
+{"id":"ntm-a8q","title":"Implement --robot-ack for send confirmation","description":"Track and confirm message receipt by agents.

## Rationale
After --robot-send, I need to know:
1. Did the agent receive the message?
2. Did the agent acknowledge understanding?
3. Is the agent actively working on it?

Without this, I'm blind after sending - I have to poll tail output and parse for confirmation patterns.

## Proposal: Ack Tracking
When sending with --robot-send --track, ntm:
1. Watches pane output for acknowledgment patterns
2. Returns structured confirmation

## Output Structure
```json
{
  "session": "myproject",
  "sent_at": "ISO8601",
  "confirmations": [
    {
      "pane": "0.1",
      "ack_type": "prompt_returned",  // agent showed ready prompt
      "ack_at": "ISO8601",
      "latency_ms": 234
    },
    {
      "pane": "0.2",
      "ack_type": "echo_detected",    // agent echoed input
      "ack_at": "ISO8601",
      "latency_ms": 156
    }
  ],
  "pending": ["0.3"],  // still waiting
  "failed": []
}
```

## Ack Detection Patterns
- prompt_returned: Agent shows ready prompt after processing
- echo_detected: Input echoed in pane (basic confirmation)
- explicit_ack: Agent responds with understood/acknowledged
- output_started: Agent begins producing output

## Timeout Handling
--timeout=30s - wait up to 30s for acks
Returns partial result with pending list if timeout","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-10T02:58:03.223434-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T02:58:03.223434-05:00"}
 {"id":"ntm-bcr","title":"EPIC: Session Presets (Recipes)","description":"","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-10T02:39:13.104435-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T02:39:13.104435-05:00","comments":[{"id":6,"issue_id":"ntm-bcr","author":"jemanuel","text":"-","created_at":"2025-12-10T07:42:01Z"}]}
 {"id":"ntm-bh5","title":"EPIC: Session Persistence & Recovery","description":"Sessions survive reboots and can be recreated from saved state.

## Problem Statement
Tmux sessions are ephemeral - a reboot or accidental kill loses everything:
- Agent counts, types, and arrangements must be manually recreated
- Working directory and project context is lost
- No way to 'bookmark' a useful session configuration
- Users avoid rebooting to preserve valuable sessions

## Goals
1. Capture complete session state (agents, layout, cwd, env)
2. Enable session recreation from saved state
3. Support periodic auto-snapshots for safety
4. Make session configs portable across machines

## Success Criteria
- `ntm session save` captures full session state to disk
- `ntm session restore` recreates session exactly
- Auto-snapshot on session creation (optional)
- `ntm session list --saved` shows available saved sessions

## Technical Approach
- Session state stored as JSON in ~/.local/share/ntm/sessions/
- Capture: session name, cwd, agent types/counts, layout, pane titles
- Restore: create session, spawn agents, apply layout
- Handle conflicts (session already exists) gracefully

## State Schema
```json
{
  "name": "myproject",
  "cwd": "/path/to/project",
  "agents": {"cc": 2, "cod": 1, "gmi": 1},
  "layout": "tiled",
  "panes": [{"title": "myproject__cc_1", "command": "claude"}],
  "created": "2025-12-10T10:00:00Z",
  "saved": "2025-12-10T12:00:00Z"
}
```

## User Value
- Fearless reboots - sessions are recoverable
- Consistent project setups across machines
- Reduced friction starting work each day
- Backup for accidental session kills","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-10T02:37:01.748915-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T02:37:01.748915-05:00"}
 {"id":"ntm-bh5.1","title":"Session State Capture","description":"Capture complete session state to JSON for later restoration.

## State to Capture
```go
type SessionState struct {
    // Identity
    Name      string    `json:"name"`
    SavedAt   time.Time `json:"saved_at"`
    
    // Context
    WorkDir   string    `json:"cwd"`
    GitBranch string    `json:"git_branch,omitempty"`
    GitRemote string    `json:"git_remote,omitempty"`
    
    // Agent Configuration
    Agents    AgentSpec `json:"agents"`  // {cc: 2, cod: 1, gmi: 1}
    
    // Pane Details (for exact recreation)
    Panes     []PaneState `json:"panes"`
    
    // Layout
    Layout    string    `json:"layout"`  // "tiled", "even-horizontal", etc.
    
    // Metadata
    CreatedAt time.Time `json:"created_at"`  // Original session creation
    Version   int       `json:"version"`     // Schema version for migrations
}

type PaneState struct {
    Title     string `json:"title"`      // e.g., "myproject__cc_1"
    Index     int    `json:"index"`
    AgentType string `json:"agent_type"` // "cc", "cod", "gmi", "user"
    Command   string `json:"command"`    // The agent launch command
    Active    bool   `json:"active"`     // Was this the active pane?
}
```

## Capture Process
1. Get session info from tmux
2. Get all panes with metadata
3. Detect working directory (from pane 0 or explicit)
4. Get git context if in repo
5. Serialize to JSON
6. Write to storage location

## Command Interface
```bash
ntm session save                 # Save current session (in tmux)
ntm session save myproject       # Save specific session
ntm session save --name=alias    # Save with custom name
ntm session save --overwrite     # Overwrite existing save
```

## Storage
Location: ~/.local/share/ntm/sessions/<name>.json

Directory structure:
```
~/.local/share/ntm/sessions/
├── myproject.json
├── frontend.json
└── backend.json
```

## Implementation
```go
func CaptureSessionState(sessionName string) (*SessionState, error) {
    session, err := tmux.GetSession(sessionName)
    if err \!= nil {
        return nil, err
    }
    
    panes, err := tmux.GetPanes(sessionName)
    if err \!= nil {
        return nil, err
    }
    
    // Extract agent configuration from pane names
    agents := countAgents(panes)
    
    // Get working directory
    cwd := detectWorkDir(panes)
    
    // Get git info if applicable
    gitBranch, gitRemote := getGitInfo(cwd)
    
    return &SessionState{
        Name:      sessionName,
        SavedAt:   time.Now(),
        WorkDir:   cwd,
        GitBranch: gitBranch,
        GitRemote: gitRemote,
        Agents:    agents,
        Panes:     mapPaneStates(panes),
        Layout:    session.Layout,
        CreatedAt: session.Created,
        Version:   1,
    }, nil
}
```

## Files to Create
- internal/session/state.go - State data structures
- internal/session/capture.go - Capture logic
- internal/session/storage.go - File I/O","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-10T02:41:31.459939-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T02:41:31.459939-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-bh5.1","depends_on_id":"ntm-bh5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-10T02:41:31.46201-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"ntm-r1l","title":"Add fsnotify-based file watcher to internal/watcher","description":"","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-10T02:43:40.366481-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T02:43:40.366481-05:00"}
 {"id":"ntm-rlp","title":"Implement alert generation and tracking","description":"Generate actionable alerts from state analysis.

## Rationale
Alerts surface problems proactively. Instead of polling and analyzing raw state, the orchestrator gets pre-computed alerts.

## Alert Types
1. **Agent Stuck**: No output for N minutes
2. **Agent Crashed**: Pane process exited
3. **High CPU**: Agent consuming excessive resources
4. **Disk Low**: Running low on disk space
5. **Bead Stale**: In-progress bead with no activity
6. **Mail Backlog**: Many unread messages
7. **Dependency Cycle**: Bead dependency loop detected

## Alert Structure
```go
type Alert struct {
    ID        string    // unique alert ID
    Type      string    // agent_stuck, disk_low, etc.
    Severity  string    // info, warning, error, critical
    Message   string    // human-readable description
    Context   map[string]interface{} // relevant data
    CreatedAt time.Time
    ResolvedAt *time.Time
}
```

## Alert Lifecycle
1. Condition detected → Alert created
2. Alert included in snapshot/health output
3. Condition resolved → Alert marked resolved
4. Resolved alerts pruned after configurable time

## Configurable Thresholds
Via config file:
```toml
[alerts]
agent_stuck_minutes = 5
disk_low_threshold_gb = 5
mail_backlog_threshold = 10
```","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-10T02:54:13.05345-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T02:54:13.05345-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-rlp","depends_on_id":"ntm-y44","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-10T02:54:27.512569-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"ntm-rm4","title":"Add --json support to ntm status command","description":"Implement JSON output for the status command.

## What This Task Does

Modify the ntm status command to support --json flag for detailed session state.

## JSON Schema

{
  "session": "myproject",
  "exists": true,
  "attached": false,
  "window_count": 1,
  "panes": [
    {
      "index": 0,
      "title": "myproject__cc_1",
      "agent_type": "claude",
      "agent_number": 1,
      "dimensions": {"width": 80, "height": 24},
      "pid": 12345,
      "command": "claude --dangerously-skip-permissions",
      "active": true
    }
  ],
  "agents": {
    "claude": 2,
    "codex": 1,
    "gemini": 0
  },
  "working_directory": "/path/to/project",
  "created_at": "2025-12-10T10:00:00Z"
}

## Implementation

1. Import internal/output package
2. Create SessionStatusOutput and PaneInfo structs
3. Modify runStatus to build structured data
4. Include all pane details in JSON
5. Parse agent type from pane titles

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] ntm status myproject --json produces valid JSON
- [ ] All pane information is included
- [ ] Agent type parsed from title
- [ ] Non-existent session returns error JSON
- [ ] Text output unchanged
","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-10T02:47:04.853845-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T02:47:04.853845-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-rm4","depends_on_id":"ntm-ws2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-10T02:47:04.854834-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
+{"id":"ntm-sc6","title":"Implement agent progress detection","description":"Detect and report agent progress through work items.

## Rationale
When an agent is working on a task, I need to understand progress:
- Is it just starting?
- Is it midway through?
- Is it finishing up?
- Has it encountered a blocker?

This enables smarter orchestration (don't interrupt agents near completion, reassign stuck work, etc.)

## Progress Indicators
Detect progress from pane output patterns:

### Starting Indicators
- 'Let me', 'I'll', 'Starting'
- File reads, searches
- Planning/analysis output

### Working Indicators
- File edits
- Code blocks being written
- Test runs
- Build commands

### Finishing Indicators
- 'Done', 'Complete', 'Finished'
- Summary output
- Return to prompt
- Commit messages

### Stuck Indicators
- 'Error', 'Failed', 'Cannot'
- Repeated attempts
- Questions asked
- Long pauses

## Output in Snapshot
```json
{
  "agents": [{
    "pane": "0.1",
    "progress": {
      "stage": "working",     // starting, working, finishing, stuck
      "confidence": 0.75,
      "indicators": ["file_edits", "code_blocks"],
      "time_in_stage_sec": 120
    }
  }]
}
```

## Future: ML-based Detection
Could train a classifier on agent outputs to detect progress more accurately. For now, heuristic-based is sufficient.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-10T02:58:19.308214-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T02:58:19.308214-05:00"}
 {"id":"ntm-sim","title":"Implement --robot-snapshot for unified state query","description":"Core orchestration primitive: single command returns complete system state including all sessions, agent health, bead summary, mail status, and alerts. This is THE most important flag because it eliminates the need for multiple queries.

## Rationale
When orchestrating N agents across M sessions working on P beads with Q mail threads, I currently need separate queries for each dimension. This compounds latency (4-6 tool calls) and burns tokens on redundant parsing. A unified snapshot gives me complete situational awareness in one atomic read.

## Output Structure
```json
{
  "ts": "ISO8601",
  "sessions": [{
    "name": "string",
    "agents": [{
      "pane": "window.pane",
      "type": "claude|codex|gemini|cursor|windsurf|aider",
      "state": "active|idle|stuck|waiting",
      "last_output_age_sec": number,
      "output_tail_lines": number,
      "current_bead": "bead-id|null",
      "pending_mail": number
    }]
  }],
  "beads_summary": {"open": N, "in_progress": N, "blocked": N, "ready": N},
  "mail_unread": number,
  "alerts": ["string"]
}
```

## Key Design Decisions
- Always include everything (no optional fields that require follow-up)
- Use compact field names (abbreviations OK for frequently-accessed data)
- Include computed fields (last_output_age_sec vs raw timestamp)
- Alerts are pre-filtered to actionable items only

## Integration Points
- tmux: session/pane enumeration, output capture
- beads: bd list --json integration  
- agent-mail: inbox count per agent
- pane title parsing for agent type detection","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-10T02:50:07.458116-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T02:50:07.458116-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-sim","depends_on_id":"ntm-y44","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-10T02:54:26.544338-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}],"comments":[{"id":10,"issue_id":"ntm-sim","author":"jemanuel","text":"## Implementation Architecture

### File Structure
```
internal/robot/
├── robot.go          # Existing - add Snapshot type and function
├── snapshot.go       # NEW - SnapshotOutput, BuildSnapshot()
├── integrations.go   # NEW - BeadsIntegration, MailIntegration
└── alerts.go         # NEW - AlertManager, Alert types
```

### Core Type
```go
type SnapshotOutput struct {
    Timestamp   time.Time              `json:"ts"`
    Sessions    []SessionSnapshot      `json:"sessions"`
    Beads       *BeadsSnapshot         `json:"beads,omitempty"`
    AgentMail   *MailSnapshot          `json:"agent_mail,omitempty"`
    Alerts      []Alert                `json:"alerts"`
    Summary     SnapshotSummary        `json:"summary"`
}
```

### Flag Registration
In root.go init():
```go
rootCmd.Flags().Bool("robot-snapshot", false, "Output complete system state as JSON")
rootCmd.Flags().String("since", "", "Return only changes since timestamp (ISO8601)")
```

### Performance Target
- Cold snapshot: <100ms
- With caching: <20ms
- Memory: <5MB for 10 sessions with 50 panes","created_at":"2025-12-10T07:55:34Z"}]}
 {"id":"ntm-spa","title":"Create internal/hooks package with config loader","description":"","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-10T02:41:28.298189-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T02:41:28.298189-05:00"}
 {"id":"ntm-ube","title":"Add adaptive theme support for light/dark terminals","description":"","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-10T02:43:02.924968-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T02:43:02.924968-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-ube","depends_on_id":"ntm-81e","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-10T02:43:16.266728-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

@@ -0,0 +1,384 @@
+// Package robot provides machine-readable output for AI agents and automation.
+// Use --robot-* flags to get JSON output suitable for piping to other tools.
+package robot
+
+import (
+	"encoding/json"
+	"fmt"
+	"os"
+	"runtime"
+	"time"
+
+	"github.com/Dicklesworthstone/ntm/internal/tmux"
+)
+
+// Build info - these will be set by the caller from cli package
+var (
+	Version = "dev"
+	Commit  = "none"
+	Date    = "unknown"
+	BuiltBy = "unknown"
+)
+
+// SessionInfo contains machine-readable session information
+type SessionInfo struct {
+	Name      string     `json:"name"`
+	Exists    bool       `json:"exists"`
+	Attached  bool       `json:"attached,omitempty"`
+	Windows   int        `json:"windows,omitempty"`
+	Panes     int        `json:"panes,omitempty"`
+	CreatedAt *time.Time `json:"created_at,omitempty"`
+	Agents    []Agent    `json:"agents,omitempty"`
+}
+
+// Agent represents an AI agent in a session
+type Agent struct {
+	Type     string `json:"type"` // claude, codex, gemini
+	Pane     string `json:"pane"`
+	Window   int    `json:"window"`
+	PaneIdx  int    `json:"pane_idx"`
+	IsActive bool   `json:"is_active"`
+}
+
+// SystemInfo contains system and runtime information
+type SystemInfo struct {
+	Version   string `json:"version"`
+	Commit    string `json:"commit"`
+	BuildDate string `json:"build_date"`
+	GoVersion string `json:"go_version"`
+	OS        string `json:"os"`
+	Arch      string `json:"arch"`
+	TmuxOK    bool   `json:"tmux_available"`
+}
+
+// StatusOutput is the structured output for robot-status
+type StatusOutput struct {
+	GeneratedAt time.Time     `json:"generated_at"`
+	System      SystemInfo    `json:"system"`
+	Sessions    []SessionInfo `json:"sessions"`
+	Summary     StatusSummary `json:"summary"`
+}
+
+// StatusSummary provides aggregate stats
+type StatusSummary struct {
+	TotalSessions   int `json:"total_sessions"`
+	TotalAgents     int `json:"total_agents"`
+	AttachedCount   int `json:"attached_count"`
+	ClaudeCount     int `json:"claude_count"`
+	CodexCount      int `json:"codex_count"`
+	GeminiCount     int `json:"gemini_count"`
+	CursorCount     int `json:"cursor_count"`
+	WindsurfCount   int `json:"windsurf_count"`
+	AiderCount      int `json:"aider_count"`
+}
+
+// PlanOutput provides an execution plan for what can be done
+type PlanOutput struct {
+	GeneratedAt   time.Time    `json:"generated_at"`
+	Recommendation string      `json:"recommendation"`
+	Actions       []PlanAction `json:"actions"`
+	Warnings      []string     `json:"warnings,omitempty"`
+}
+
+// PlanAction is a suggested action
+type PlanAction struct {
+	Priority    int      `json:"priority"` // 1=high, 2=medium, 3=low
+	Command     string   `json:"command"`
+	Description string   `json:"description"`
+	Args        []string `json:"args,omitempty"`
+}
+
+// PrintHelp outputs AI agent help documentation
+func PrintHelp() {
+	help := `ntm (Named Tmux Manager) AI Agent Interface
+=============================================
+This tool helps AI agents manage tmux sessions with multiple coding assistants.
+
+Commands for AI Agents:
+-----------------------
+
+--robot-status
+    Outputs JSON with all session information and agent counts.
+    Key fields:
+    - sessions: Array of active sessions with their agents
+    - summary: Aggregate counts (total_agents, claude_count, etc.)
+    - system: Version, OS, tmux availability
+
+--robot-plan
+    Outputs a recommended execution plan based on current state.
+    Key fields:
+    - recommendation: What to do first
+    - actions: Prioritized list of commands to run
+    - warnings: Issues that need attention
+
+--robot-sessions
+    Outputs minimal session list for quick lookup.
+    Faster than --robot-status when you only need names.
+
+--robot-version
+    Outputs version info as JSON.
+
+Common Workflows:
+-----------------
+
+1) Start a coding session:
+   ntm spawn myproject --cc=2 --cod=1 --gem=1 --json
+
+2) Check session state:
+   ntm status --robot-status
+
+3) Send a prompt to all agents:
+   ntm send myproject --all "implement feature X"
+
+4) Get output from a specific agent:
+   ntm copy myproject:1 --last=50
+
+Tips for AI Agents:
+-------------------
+- Use --json flag on spawn/create for structured output
+- Parse ntm status --robot-status to understand current state
+- Use ntm send --all for broadcast, --pane=N for targeted
+- Output is always UTF-8 JSON, one object per line where applicable
+`
+	fmt.Println(help)
+}
+
+// PrintStatus outputs machine-readable status
+func PrintStatus() error {
+	output := StatusOutput{
+		GeneratedAt: time.Now().UTC(),
+		System: SystemInfo{
+			Version:   Version,
+			Commit:    Commit,
+			BuildDate: Date,
+			GoVersion: runtime.Version(),
+			OS:        runtime.GOOS,
+			Arch:      runtime.GOARCH,
+			TmuxOK:    tmux.IsInstalled(),
+		},
+		Sessions: []SessionInfo{},
+		Summary:  StatusSummary{},
+	}
+
+	// Get all sessions
+	sessions, err := tmux.ListSessions()
+	if err != nil {
+		// tmux not running is not an error for status
+		return encodeJSON(output)
+	}
+
+	for _, sess := range sessions {
+		info := SessionInfo{
+			Name:     sess.Name,
+			Exists:   true,
+			Attached: sess.Attached,
+			Windows:  sess.Windows,
+		}
+
+		// Try to get agents from panes
+		panes, err := tmux.GetPanes(sess.Name)
+		if err == nil {
+			info.Panes = len(panes)
+			for _, pane := range panes {
+				agent := Agent{
+					Pane:     pane.ID,
+					Window:   0, // GetPanes doesn't include window index
+					PaneIdx:  pane.Index,
+					IsActive: pane.Active,
+				}
+
+				// Try to detect agent type from pane title or content
+				agent.Type = detectAgentType(pane.Title)
+				info.Agents = append(info.Agents, agent)
+
+				// Update summary counts
+				switch agent.Type {
+				case "claude":
+					output.Summary.ClaudeCount++
+				case "codex":
+					output.Summary.CodexCount++
+				case "gemini":
+					output.Summary.GeminiCount++
+				case "cursor":
+					output.Summary.CursorCount++
+				case "windsurf":
+					output.Summary.WindsurfCount++
+				case "aider":
+					output.Summary.AiderCount++
+				}
+				output.Summary.TotalAgents++
+			}
+		}
+
+		output.Sessions = append(output.Sessions, info)
+		output.Summary.TotalSessions++
+		if sess.Attached {
+			output.Summary.AttachedCount++
+		}
+	}
+
+	return encodeJSON(output)
+}
+
+// PrintVersion outputs version as JSON
+func PrintVersion() error {
+	info := struct {
+		Version   string `json:"version"`
+		Commit    string `json:"commit"`
+		BuildDate string `json:"build_date"`
+		BuiltBy   string `json:"built_by"`
+		GoVersion string `json:"go_version"`
+		OS        string `json:"os"`
+		Arch      string `json:"arch"`
+	}{
+		Version:   Version,
+		Commit:    Commit,
+		BuildDate: Date,
+		BuiltBy:   BuiltBy,
+		GoVersion: runtime.Version(),
+		OS:        runtime.GOOS,
+		Arch:      runtime.GOARCH,
+	}
+	return encodeJSON(info)
+}
+
+// PrintSessions outputs minimal session list
+func PrintSessions() error {
+	sessions, err := tmux.ListSessions()
+	if err != nil {
+		return encodeJSON([]SessionInfo{})
+	}
+
+	output := make([]SessionInfo, 0, len(sessions))
+	for _, sess := range sessions {
+		output = append(output, SessionInfo{
+			Name:     sess.Name,
+			Exists:   true,
+			Attached: sess.Attached,
+			Windows:  sess.Windows,
+		})
+	}
+	return encodeJSON(output)
+}
+
+// PrintPlan outputs an execution plan
+func PrintPlan() error {
+	plan := PlanOutput{
+		GeneratedAt: time.Now().UTC(),
+		Actions:     []PlanAction{},
+	}
+
+	// Check tmux availability
+	if !tmux.IsInstalled() {
+		plan.Recommendation = "Install tmux first"
+		plan.Warnings = append(plan.Warnings, "tmux is not installed or not in PATH")
+		plan.Actions = append(plan.Actions, PlanAction{
+			Priority:    1,
+			Command:     "brew install tmux",
+			Description: "Install tmux using Homebrew (macOS)",
+		})
+		return encodeJSON(plan)
+	}
+
+	// Check for existing sessions
+	sessions, _ := tmux.ListSessions()
+
+	if len(sessions) == 0 {
+		plan.Recommendation = "Create your first coding session"
+		plan.Actions = append(plan.Actions, PlanAction{
+			Priority:    1,
+			Command:     "ntm spawn myproject --cc=2",
+			Description: "Create session with 2 Claude Code agents",
+			Args:        []string{"spawn", "myproject", "--cc=2"},
+		})
+		plan.Actions = append(plan.Actions, PlanAction{
+			Priority:    2,
+			Command:     "ntm tutorial",
+			Description: "Learn NTM with an interactive tutorial",
+			Args:        []string{"tutorial"},
+		})
+	} else {
+		plan.Recommendation = "Attach to an existing session or create a new one"
+
+		// Find unattached sessions
+		for _, sess := range sessions {
+			if !sess.Attached {
+				plan.Actions = append(plan.Actions, PlanAction{
+					Priority:    1,
+					Command:     fmt.Sprintf("ntm attach %s", sess.Name),
+					Description: fmt.Sprintf("Attach to session '%s' (%d windows)", sess.Name, sess.Windows),
+					Args:        []string{"attach", sess.Name},
+				})
+			}
+		}
+
+		plan.Actions = append(plan.Actions, PlanAction{
+			Priority:    2,
+			Command:     "ntm palette",
+			Description: "Open command palette for quick actions",
+			Args:        []string{"palette"},
+		})
+		plan.Actions = append(plan.Actions, PlanAction{
+			Priority:    3,
+			Command:     "ntm dashboard",
+			Description: "Open visual session dashboard",
+			Args:        []string{"dashboard"},
+		})
+	}
+
+	return encodeJSON(plan)
+}
+
+func detectAgentType(title string) string {
+	// Try to detect from pane title
+	switch {
+	case contains(title, "claude"):
+		return "claude"
+	case contains(title, "codex"):
+		return "codex"
+	case contains(title, "gemini"):
+		return "gemini"
+	case contains(title, "cursor"):
+		return "cursor"
+	case contains(title, "windsurf"):
+		return "windsurf"
+	case contains(title, "aider"):
+		return "aider"
+	default:
+		return "unknown"
+	}
+}
+
+func contains(s, substr string) bool {
+	return len(s) >= len(substr) && (s == substr ||
+		len(s) > 0 && containsLower(s, substr))
+}
+
+func containsLower(s, substr string) bool {
+	s = toLower(s)
+	substr = toLower(substr)
+	for i := 0; i <= len(s)-len(substr); i++ {
+		if s[i:i+len(substr)] == substr {
+			return true
+		}
+	}
+	return false
+}
+
+func toLower(s string) string {
+	b := make([]byte, len(s))
+	for i := 0; i < len(s); i++ {
+		c := s[i]
+		if c >= 'A' && c <= 'Z' {
+			c += 'a' - 'A'
+		}
+		b[i] = c
+	}
+	return string(b)
+}
+
+func encodeJSON(v interface{}) error {
+	encoder := json.NewEncoder(os.Stdout)
+	encoder.SetIndent("", "  ")
+	return encoder.Encode(v)
+}
